title: Temporal Friction
body: In the administrative governance of clinical research, does the delay between trial initiation and public posting indicate a significant regional divide in regulatory viscosity and transparency? This metadata audit evaluated temporal delays for 800 trials across Africa, Europe, China, and India using the start-date and first-post-date fields from ClinicalTrials.gov. Researchers performed a registration-velocity analysis and reported the median posting delay as the primary comparative estimand for research transparency and administrative efficiency. The primary result showed a negative median delay in Europe and Africa, indicating a proactive protocol-first registration culture in these regions during the audit. Conversely, China and India exhibited significant positive delays exceeding ninety days, suggesting a retrospective enrollment-first approach to clinical trial registration and reporting. These findings reveal hidden cultural and regulatory differences in how research transparency is practiced across diverse global innovation hubs. Interpretation is limited by the reliance on self-reported start dates which may be subject to sponsor recall bias. - DOI: 10.156/temporal-friction - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research - Mode: topological
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
